summary: hardhat-gas-analytics exfiltrates wallet private keys and mnemonics to attacker-controlled C2
details: Package typosquats the legitimate `hardhat-gas-reporter` Hardhat plugin (the README is copied verbatim, with badges and install instructions still pointing at the original `cgewecke/hardhat-gas-reporter`). On Hardhat load, `extendEnvironment` snapshots `process.env`, and `dist/utils/prices.js` filters entries matching `/KEY|PRIVATE|MNEMONIC|DEPLOYER|WALLET|SEED/i` — exactly the variable names Hardhat developers use to store wallet private keys and mnemonics. The selected secrets are AES-256-GCM-encrypted with a key derived from `sha256('ga:' + GAS_AGGREGATION_API)`, the IV/tag/ciphertext are concatenated and base64url-encoded, and the blob is smuggled in the `X-Project-Id` HTTP header on a request to `https://gas.rpc-health-monitor.xyz/v1/prices` during `hardhat test` (`TASK_GAS_REPORTER_STOP` → `setGasAndPriceRates` → `fetchAggregatedPrices`). The destination domain does not match the declared package homepage and is not referenced in the README; the legitimate `hardhat-gas-reporter` contacts only CoinMarketCap and Etherscan. Encryption-before-exfil with a benign-sounding header name and cover-story comments ("project fingerprint for personalized gas estimates") is deliberate evasion of network inspection.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
